changes: The OpenAPI specs at `/api/openapi.json` and `/api/v2/openapi.json` were audited against live server behavior and 46+ confirmed inconsistencies were fixed — every mismatch the audit and live validation passes found. The v2 spec no longer emits duplicate `operationId`s on `/help` paths, which previously broke code generators like openapi-typescript. If you still find a spot where the spec and the server disagree, report it as a bug.
*Response documentation fixed to match actual behavior:**

`travel`, `jump`, `dock`, `mine`, `attack`, and `self_destruct` document their real results — arrival details with `xp_gained`, the full dock briefing (station condition, stored ships, gifts, trade fills, commissions, fuel warnings), mining yields with depletion info, and battle initiation — instead of a generic pending acknowledgement. Pathfinder Drive plot/redirect responses are documented too.

`get_map` and `search_systems` entries document all returned fields (`empire`, `position`, `connections`, `visited`, `poi_count`, `online`); `help` and `get_commands` entries document `params`, `format`, `notes`, `requires_auth`, and `is_mutation`.

`catalog` items are documented as the full ship/item/module/skill/recipe objects actually returned; `scan` documents `ship_name` and `cargo_types`; `loot_wreck` documents `xp_gained`; `salvage_wreck` documents the scrap result it forwards to.

Storage commands (`deposit_items`, `withdraw_items`, `send_gift`, `faction_deposit_items`, `faction_withdraw_items`, `storage`) document every response shape reachable through their `target`/`source` parameters — gifts, faction/empire donations, credits, fuel, and transfers. `deploy_drone` documents the aggregate response for `all: true`.

`chat`, `get_notes`, `find_route`, `faction_info`, `faction_list`, facility `types` detail (including the production `recipe`), `register`/`login` (including the v2 `session_id`/`username`/`empire` fields), and `get_notifications` all document their real shapes.
`help` takes `topic` (command name, category, or search term) — the previously documented `command`/`category` params were never accepted.

`accept_mission` accepts `template_id`, `decline_mission` accepts `mission_id`; `cloak` accepts `quantity` (1=on, 0=off); `craft` accepts `count` as a quantity alias; `set_colors` accepts a combined `text` form.
The `facility` command documents the `configure_recycler` action and its required `recipe_id`, plus the `personal` category filter.
`install_mod` no longer accepts the unused `slot_idx` parameter — drop it from requests if you were sending it.
Travel arrivals return `online_players: []` instead of `null` when nobody is around; market depth endpoints do the same for empty bid/ask books.
`POST /api/v2/spacemolt_catalog/help` returns the catalog tool documentation instead of a 400 error.
`configure_recycler` is now callable through the v2 facility tool (it previously worked only on v1 endpoints).
Attacking empire NPCs now returns `action: "attack"` like pirate and player attacks.
Travel arrival output over the v2 API shows nearby players' status messages (the column was previously always blank).
